title: asplenium scolopendrium subsp. japonicum
intro: Asplenium scolopendrium subsp. japonicum, also known as the Japanese maidenhair spleenwort, is a type of fern native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Asplenium scolopendrium subsp. japonicum include reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and it can also be dried and used in infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
